_note: generated by tools/genvectors/gen_vectors.py -- do not edit
note: Ranging-attempt idempotency conformance (PROTOCOL_V2.md §10:379-382, §12:469-470). ni_peer ranging_offer/accept/start/stop are the (sid, attemptId)-keyed operations, exchanged iOS<->iOS; Android is always the OOB controller and treats them as no-ops. 'role' is the local RangingCoordinator role; an 'offerer' emits ranging_offer(attemptId=1) on start, before any op is fed. 'expectOutbound' is the exact message stream the coordinator emits; 'sessionsStarted'/'sessionsStopped' are the deduped session effects.

cases.name: duplicateRangingStart
cases.note: duplicate ranging_start (same sid+attemptId) -> single session effect

cases.name: duplicateRangingStop
cases.note: duplicate ranging_stop -> no error; the second stop is a no-op

cases.name: staleAttemptIgnored
cases.note: an accept for a non-current attemptId is ignored (no ranging_start for a stale/unknown attempt)
